channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish has not been collected in weeks, 7th July
item.description:
Rubbish has not been collected in weeks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kingsland Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There is lots of litter in the floor and the non-recycled rubbish has not been collected, 7th July
item.description:
There is a lot of litter on the floor, and the non-recycled rubbish has not been collected In 1 Dunston road in over 3 weeks so the smell is really bad

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kingsland Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Van appears abandoned parked in Dellfield Close, 7th July
item.description:
This van has been left parked on a narrow cul-de-sac road for around 2 months. It is unsightly with an accumulation of rubbish inside and is not owned by any residents. It is also illegally parked given it has no current MOT ( already reported to authorities).

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dellfield Close, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter picking and sweeping required, 7th July
item.description:
Lots of rubbish near the curb on Radnor rd, Se15 on the stretch from Freda Corbet, Sister Mabel's and towards the junction with Commercial Way. This hasn't been swept for a long time. Please assist. Thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 36, Radnor Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Chairs on pavement, 7th July
item.description:
Chairs on pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Warwick Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Growing pile of rubbish, 7th July
item.description:
Second day reported in a row. We need our bin back. We need this rubbish collected. This is a serious health issue

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Llanover Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Too many lime bikes spilling over onto rest of the road, 7th July
item.description:
This is the busiest part of Balmes Road and these Lime bikes have not been moved even after informing lime several hours ago. There are more parked since and they are on either side of this busy junction. Cars are having reverse and forth to make the tight turn make worse by the bikes. This is not a suitable place to site a bike parking bay. It would be much better to move it further down the road and make a bigger parking site.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 18, Southgate Road, Hoxton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on wall next to bus stop at start of Anerley Park, 7th July
item.description:
Graffiti on the wall nearby the bus stop at the start of Anerley Park. Please can this be removed. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 107-111, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bike here since Saturday 4th July, 7th July
item.description:
Forerst bike here since Saturday 4rh July

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29, Seymour Road, Harringay, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumping of household waste and rubble into commercial premises bins, 7th July
item.description:
Someone that was captured on cctv stole our commercial waste bins and filled them with household waste, happened around 6pm on Tuesday 7th July 2026 on Thrale Street. Left identifying items in the bins.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 53-61, Southwark Bridge Road, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Extremely hazardous waste situation with piling up of rubbish in to street. Foul odours and pests., 7th July
item.description:
A bin which has been flytipped waste all around it and piling up on the street is now a health hazard. It smells vile from down the road as it’s all rotting in the heat with flies/pests are all around it. This is dangerous waste and needs immediate resolving.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 177-179, Southwark Bridge Road, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on street sign at the start of Anerley Park, 7th July
item.description:
Graffiti on the Anerley Park street sign. Please can this be removed. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 107-111, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on bench, 7th July
item.description:
Graffiti on the bench near Anerley Park on the corner. Please can this be removed. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 107-111, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree blocking entire pavement, 7th July
item.description:
The tree needs trimming as the branches and leaves are blocking the entire pavement causing you to walk on someone’s driveway to avoid it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44, Avondale Avenue, East Barnet, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 7th July
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bellefields Road, Brixton, Greater London

item.title: Street light not working outside 99 Latchmere road, 7th July
item.description:
Street light has been not working for about a month or so
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 97, Latchmere Road, Clapham Junction, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Remove parkbench to stop congregating leading to excessive anti social behaviour, 7th July
item.description:
A public bench in [name of park/street] is sited too close to residential properties — approximately 5-10 metres from the nearest homes. Because of its position, it has become a regular late-night gathering spot for groups playing loud music and drinking alcohol, typically between 23:00 and 02:00. This is now a repeat occurrence, not a one-off. Most recently, on 16 June 2026, a group gathered at the bench playing loud music and drinking from around midnight. The Metropolitan Police were called at 00:30 (CAD reference 513/16JUN26), and the disturbance continued until approximately 02:00. Residents were kept awake into the early hours. The core issue is the bench's location. Its proximity to housing means any late-night gathering there causes direct and unavoidable noise disturbance to residents, and the same pattern keeps repeating regardless of individual police call-outs. Reporting each incident deals only with the symptom; the siting of the bench is the underlying cause. We are asking the council to review the siting of this bench and relocate or remove it, in addition to any enforcement action against the anti-social behaviour itself. An incident diary with dates, times and police references is being maintained and is available on request.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rope Street, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Drain is choked with dirt and vegetation, 7th July
item.description:
The drain is completely blocked.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Excess litter penshurst Road, 7th July
item.description:
Penshurst Rd is currently covered in litter, with rubbish outside almost every house along the entire length of the road. There is also broken glass on the pavement, creating a safety hazard for residents, children and pets. No street cleaners.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Penshurst Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped fridge blocking path, 7th July
item.description:
This house got a new fridge and dumped the old fridge outside, blocking the footpath. They also dumped the polystyrene packaging which also scattered throughout the estate. They also park their Grey Volvo too far back and further block the path.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Grand Union Crescent, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
